rulesStrings.li: overview->Recorded by [artist], this [format] blends [genre] with [genre2] influences
overview->The liner notes credit [artist] as the sole composer and performer
overview->A collaborative [format] from the [worldname] music scene
production->The liner notes say it was pressed in limited quantities by a small label on [worldname]
production->According to the [disc_location], it was self-released by [artist] after a string of rejections
production->The [disc_location] says the recording was made in a single overnight session
production->The liner notes indicate this was the last thing [artist] ever recorded
production->A stamped note on the [disc_location] says this was one of only fifty copies pressed
production->The [disc_location] notes that all master recordings were lost in a fire, making this a rare survivor
production->According to the [disc_location], the original pressing sold poorly but became sought after later
production->The [disc_location] claims this was recorded in a single room with no professional equipment
production->A handwritten note on the [disc_location] says copies were distributed free of charge
production->The [disc_location] credits over a dozen musicians, none of whom are named anywhere else
production->The [disc_location] says [artist] spent three years writing these compositions before recording them
production->According to the [disc_location], this was intended for a film that was never made
reception(quality[greater_than]4)->By most accounts it is considered a landmark recording of its era
reception(quality[greater_than]4)->The [disc_location] is filled with glowing critical praise
reception(quality[greater_than]3)->It found an appreciative audience, if not a large one
reception(quality[greater_than]3)->Most who encounter it seem to find it moving in some way
reception(quality[less_than]4)->Critics were divided, though listeners seemed warmer to it
reception(quality[less_than]4)->It passed largely without notice on release
reception(quality[less_than]3)->Reviews at the time were unkind
reception(quality[less_than]2)->It is unclear why it was ever pressed at all
reception(quality==6)->It is spoken of in hushed tones by those who know it
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] wrote the opening track during a siege
anecdote->A note inside says that [artist] refused to listen to a playback of the finished recording
anecdote->The [disc_location] mentions that [artist] was reportedly in poor health throughout the sessions
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] dedicated the [format] to someone named only as 'R'
anecdote->The [disc_location] claims [artist] was unaware the sessions were being recorded
anecdote->A scrawled note on the inner sleeve reads 'this one is for those who didn't make it'
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], the [format] was recorded in an abandoned building on [worldname]
anecdote->The [disc_location] says [artist] performed the entire [format] in one continuous take
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] never spoke publicly about this [format]
anecdote->According to the [disc_location], [artist] considered this [format] a failure and was reportedly furious it was released
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]4)->Listening to it, it is easy to understand why some consider it essential
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]4)->There is something in it that feels genuinely irreplaceable
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]3)->It holds up well, even across the distance of years
quality_statement(quality[greater_than]3)->Not everyone would call it great, but most would call it good
quality_statement(quality[less_than]4)->Its flaws are audible, but so is the effort behind it
quality_statement(quality[less_than]3)->It is more interesting as an artifact than as a listening experience
quality_statement(quality[less_than]2)->It is hard to recommend, even to completionists
